title: hex_encode/hex_decode are hand-rolled; use hex crate or const-hex
description:
In `did.rs:120-136`, hex encoding/decoding is hand-rolled:

```rust
pub fn hex_encode(bytes: &[u8]) -> String {
    bytes.iter().map(|b| format!("{b:02x}")).collect()
}
```

This is correct but allocates a new String per byte via `format!()`. The `hex` crate (or `const-hex` for const contexts) is 0-dep, faster, and well-audited. The project already has a separate `api::hex_encode` (grep shows cli.rs:306, cli.rs:676 use it) — so there are two independent hex implementations. These should be consolidated.

Affected: src/did.rs:120-136, likely also src/api.rs (duplicate)
